q: Scientific research and the development of new tools and techniques have undoubtedly improved the quality of our lives.
Scientific research and the development of new tools and techniques have undoubtedly decreased the quality of our lives.
The relevance of biology is the important of the human society and humankind.
The relevance of biology is limited only to academic study and has no importance to human society.
Biology is also important to our everyday life because it allows human beings to know better their bodies, their resources and potential threats in the environment.
Biology is important to our everyday life solely because it allows human beings to classify organisms into taxonomic groups.
Biology being the study of living things help us to understand every organism.
Biology being the study of non-living things help us to understand every organism.
Advances in medicine, agriculture, biotechnology, and many other areas of biology have brought improvements in the quality of life.
Advances in medicine, agriculture, biotechnology, and many other areas of biology have brought no improvements in the quality of life.
Fields such as genetics and evolution give insight into the past and can help shape the future.
Fields such as genetics and evolution give no insight into the past and cannot help shape the future.
Research in ecology and conservation inform how we can protect this planet's precious biodiversity.
Research in ecology and conservation has no relevance to protecting this planet's precious biodiversity.
Biology has also helped us to understand the characteristics of living things, the similarities between plants and animals, as well as how germs work and help us staying healthier.
Biology has helped us understand that plants and animals share no similarities whatsoever.
Agriculture is defined to produce commodities which maintain life, including foods, fibers, forest products, agricultural crops, horticultural crops, and their related services.
Agriculture is defined to produce only food commodities and excludes fibers, forest products, and horticultural crops.
Agricultural crops mean food or fiber commodities grown for resale or commercial or economic purposes that provide foods, clothing, or animal feeds.
Agricultural crops mean only food commodities grown for personal consumption and never for resale or commercial purposes.
The global major agricultural products can be broadly grouped into foods, fibers, fuels, and raw materials.
The global major agricultural products can be broadly grouped into only foods and fibers, excluding fuels and raw materials.
Myanmar's agricultural exports include rice, maize, blackgram, greengram, pigeonpea, chickpea, sesame, onion, tamarind, raw rubber, vegetables, and fruits.
Myanmar's agricultural exports are limited exclusively to rice and maize, with no other products.
Commercially cultivating agricultural crops in Myanmar are mainly cereals, pulses and oilseed crops.
Commercially cultivating agricultural crops in Myanmar are mainly fruits and flowers only.
Rice (Oryza sativa) production is based on its environment, resulting in rain fed lowland rice, winter rice, deep-water rice, upland rice and irrigated rice.
Rice (Oryza sativa) production is based solely on a single method regardless of environmental conditions.
The monsoon season is the main rice production season as rice paddies rely on copious amounts of water.
The dry season is the main rice production season as rice paddies require minimal amounts of water.
There are two dominant rice production systems: rain fed lowland and irrigated lowland.
There are three dominant rice production systems: rain fed lowland, irrigated lowland, and deep-water systems.
The traditional method for cultivating rice is flooding the fields while, or after, setting the young seedlings.
The traditional method for cultivating rice is draining all water from the fields before setting the young seedlings.
Wheat (Triticum aestivum) is a grass widely cultivated for its seeds, a cereal grain that is a worldwide staple food.
Wheat (Triticum aestivum) is a legume widely cultivated for its pods, a pulse that is a worldwide staple food.
The wheat can be cultivated once a year.
The wheat can be cultivated three times a year in all climates.
The zero-tillage technique is a new method of wheat cultivation that is used in the Rice-Wheat cropping system.
The zero-tillage technique is an ancient method of wheat cultivation used exclusively in monoculture systems.
The wheat crop requires a well-pulverized but compact seedbed for good and uniform germination.
The wheat crop requires a loose and uncompacted seedbed for good and uniform germination.
Maize (Zea mays) is a tropical plant that prefers warm humid weather.
Maize (Zea mays) is a temperate plant that prefers cold dry weather.
Maize is a short-day plant.
Maize is a long-day plant.
Maize is very sensitive to excess or deficit soil moisture.
Maize is tolerant of both excess and deficit soil moisture without any adverse effects.
Maize cannot withstand frost at any stage of growth.
Maize can withstand frost at all stages of growth without damage.
Seeds should not be sown more than 5-6 cm depth of soil for maize cultivation.
Seeds should be sown at least 15-20 cm depth of soil for maize cultivation.
Among the ASEAN countries, Myanmar is the leading pulse and bean producer.
Among the ASEAN countries, Thailand is the leading pulse and bean producer.
Globally, Myanmar is the third-largest producer of pulses after India and Canada.
Globally, Myanmar is the largest producer of pulses, surpassing both India and Canada.
About 18 types of pulses are produced in Myanmar, led by blackgram (Vigna mungo).
About 5 types of pulses are produced in Myanmar, led by soybean (Glycine max).
Horticulture is a branch of agriculture.
Horticulture is completely independent of agriculture and has no relationship to it.
Horticulture is defined as the applied science of cultivating and growing plants used for both consumption and aesthetic or ornamental purposes.
Horticulture is defined as the science of cultivating only food crops for commercial sale.
Horticulture involves plant propagation and cultivation to improve the plant growths, yields, quality, nutritional values, and resistance to insects, diseases, and environmental stresses.
Horticulture involves only the harvesting of wild plants without any cultivation or improvement efforts.
The word 'horticulture' is derived from the Latin words 'hortus' and 'cultura', which mean 'garden' and 'cultivation'.
The word 'horticulture' is derived from the Greek words 'horto' and 'cultus', which mean 'field' and 'harvesting'.
In contrast to agriculture, horticulture does not include the intensive crop farming and large-scale crop production or animal husbandry.
Horticulture includes intensive crop farming and large-scale crop production identical to agriculture.
Horticulture focuses on the use of small plots with a wide variety of mixed crops while agriculture focuses on one large primary crop at a time.
Agriculture focuses on small plots with mixed crops while horticulture focuses on one large primary crop at a time.
Olericulture is the vegetable growing, dealing with the culture of non-woody (herbaceous) plants for foods.
Olericulture is the production of fruits and nuts from woody plants.
Pomology or Fruticulture is the production of fruits and nuts.
Pomology or Fruticulture is the production of vegetables and herbs.
Viticulture is the production of grapes (largely intended for winemaking).
Viticulture is the production of wheat and other cereal grains.
Floriculture is the growing and marketing of flowers and ornamental plants for floristry.
Floriculture is the growing and marketing of food crops for commercial consumption.
Turf Management is the production and upkeep of turf, artificial and live, for use in recreation.
Turf Management is the production and upkeep of only artificial turf for industrial use.
Arboriculture is the cultivation and care of individual trees, shrubs, vines, and other perennial woody plants.
Arboriculture is the cultivation and care of annual herbaceous plants only.
Landscape Horticulture is the selection, production and care of plants used in landscape architecture.
Landscape Horticulture is the selection, production and care of animals used in landscape architecture.
Postharvest Physiology is the management of harvested horticultural crops to determine the best storage and transportation conditions to optimize shelf life after harvest.
Postharvest Physiology is the study of how to increase crop yields during the growing season only.
Hydroponics is a method of growing plants without soil.
Hydroponics is a method of growing plants exclusively in enriched soil.
Hydroponics uses less water than traditional soil-based systems.
Hydroponics uses more water than traditional soil-based systems.
Hydroponic growing allows for faster growth and higher yields than traditional soil-based growing systems.
Hydroponic growing results in slower growth and lower yields than traditional soil-based growing systems.
Hydroponics is a type of horticulture and a subset of hydroculture which involves growing plants, usually crops, without soil, by using water-based mineral nutrient solutions in aqueous solvents.
Hydroponics is a type of agriculture that involves growing plants in soil enriched with water-based mineral nutrient solutions.
Terrestrial or aquatic plants may grow with their roots exposed to the nutritious liquid in hydroponic systems.
Only aquatic plants can grow in hydroponic systems with their roots exposed to the nutritious liquid.
The roots may be physically supported by an inert medium such as perlite, gravel, or other substrates in hydroponic systems.
The roots in hydroponic systems must always be physically supported by nutrient-rich soil substrates.
Despite inert media, roots can cause changes of the rhizosphere pH and root exudates can affect rhizosphere biology and physiological balance of the nutrient solution by secondary metabolites.
Inert media in hydroponic systems completely prevent any changes to rhizosphere pH and root exudates have no effect on the nutrient solution.
The nutrients used in hydroponic systems can come from many different sources, including fish excrement, duck manure, purchased chemical fertilizers, or artificial nutrient solutions.
The nutrients used in hydroponic systems can only come from purchased chemical fertilizers and no other sources.
Plants are commonly grown hydroponically in a greenhouse, on inert media, include tomatoes, peppers, cucumbers, strawberries, lettuces, and cannabis, usually for commercial use.
Plants are commonly grown hydroponically only in open fields and never in greenhouses.
Arabidopsis serves as a model organism in plant science and genetics in hydroponic systems.
Arabidopsis is never used as a model organism in plant science and genetics.
Hydroponics offers many advantages, notably a decrease in water usage in agriculture.
Hydroponics offers no advantages and increases water usage in agriculture.
Aquaculture, also known as aquafarming, is the farming of aquatic organisms such as fish, crustaceans, molluscs, algae and other aquatic plants.
Aquaculture, also known as aquafarming, is the farming of terrestrial organisms such as cattle, sheep, and goats.
Aquaculture involves cultivating freshwater, brackish water and saltwater populations under controlled or semi-natural conditions.
Aquaculture involves cultivating only freshwater populations under completely natural conditions without any human intervention.
Aquaculture can be contrasted with commercial fishing, which is the harvesting of wild fish.
Aquaculture is identical to commercial fishing, which is the harvesting of wild fish.
Farming implies some sort of intervention in the rearing process to enhance production, such as regular stocking, feeding, protection from predators, etc.
Farming implies no intervention in the rearing process and allows organisms to develop completely naturally.
Fish farming or pisciculture involves commercial breeding of fish, usually for food, in fish tanks or artificial enclosures such as fish ponds.
Fish farming or pisciculture involves only the capture of wild fish from natural water bodies without any breeding.
Worldwide, the most important fish species used in fish farming are, in order, carp, salmon, tilapia and catfish.
Worldwide, the most important fish species used in fish farming are, in order, tuna, swordfish, marlin and shark.
Mariculture or marine farming is a specialized branch of aquaculture involving the cultivation of marine organisms for food and other animal products.
Mariculture or marine farming is a specialized branch of agriculture involving the cultivation of terrestrial organisms.
Shrimp farming is an aquaculture business that exists in either a marine or freshwater environment, producing shrimp or prawns for human consumption.
Shrimp farming is an aquaculture business that exists only in marine environments and never in freshwater.
Oyster farming is an aquaculture practice in which oysters are bred and raised mainly for their pearls, shells and inner organ tissue, which is eaten.
Oyster farming is an aquaculture practice in which oysters are bred and raised exclusively for decorative purposes with no food value.
Algaculture is the farming of algae species including microalgae and macroalgae.
Algaculture is the farming of only macroalgae, excluding all microalgae species.
The majority of algae that are intentionally cultivated fall into the category of microalgae.
The majority of algae that are intentionally cultivated fall into the category of macroalgae.
Macroalgae, commonly known as seaweed, also have many commercial and industrial uses.
Macroalgae, commonly known as seaweed, have no commercial or industrial uses whatsoever.
Integrated fish farming is a system of producing fish in combination with other agricultural or livestock farming operations centered on the fish pond.
Integrated fish farming is a system of producing fish in complete isolation from any other agricultural or livestock operations.
The farming sub-systems eg., fish, crop and livestock are linked to each other in such a way that the byproducts or wastes from one sub-system become the valuable inputs to another sub-system.
In integrated fish farming, the byproducts or wastes from one sub-system are discarded and never used by other sub-systems.

Aquaculture serves different purposes such as food production, restoration of threatened and endangered species populations, wild stock population enhancement, the building of aquariums, and fish cultures, and habitat restoration.
Aquaculture serves only one purpose: food production for human consumption.

The term 'Livestock' refers to any domesticated, land-living animal that is raised to provide resources like meat, milk, eggs, and feathers, or to provide services like transportation or cultivation assistance.
The term 'Livestock' refers only to wild animals that are hunted for food in their natural habitats.
The most common livestock animals are cattle, sheep, goat, pig, horse, chicken, and duck.
The most common livestock animals are elephant, tiger, lion, bear, wolf, eagle, and crocodile.
Livestock can be raised for the production of a useful form of dietary protein and energy as meat.
Livestock cannot be raised for the production of dietary protein and energy as meat.
Livestock can be used as a source of milk, other dairy products, such as yogurt, cheese, butter.
Livestock cannot be used as a source of milk or any dairy products.
Livestock produce a range of fibre or textiles, for example, sheep and goats produce wool and mohair.
Livestock produce no fibre or textiles of any kind.
Manure can be spread on fields to increase crop yields and is also used to make plaster for walls and floors and can be used as a fuel for fires.
Manure has no agricultural value and cannot be used to increase crop yields or for any other purpose.
The blood and bone of animals are also used as fertilizer.
The blood and bone of animals are never used as fertilizer and have no agricultural value.
Animals such as horses, donkey, and yaks can be used for mechanical energy and are non-human labor still using in plowing fields, transporting goods, and military function.
Animals such as horses, donkey, and yaks have never been used for mechanical energy or labor purposes.
The grazing of livestock is sometimes used as a way to control weeds and undergrowth.
The grazing of livestock always increases weeds and undergrowth without any control benefit.
Livestock may be kept for the commercial value of breeding the animals, such as in horse breeding.
Livestock are never kept for breeding purposes and have no commercial value in breeding.
Livestock may be raised for sports purposes, such as raising horses for horse racing or polo.
Livestock are never raised for sports purposes and have no role in recreational activities.
Livestock may be raised to be sold commercially as pets.
Livestock are never raised to be sold as pets.
The global demand and production of livestock products are increasing rapidly, due to the population growth, rising income, and changes in lifestyle and diets.
The global demand and production of livestock products are decreasing rapidly due to declining population and falling income levels.
Livestock are key drivers for sustainable development in agriculture.
Livestock are obstacles to sustainable development in agriculture.
Livestock contribute to food security, nutrition, poverty alleviation, and economic growth.
Livestock contribute only to economic growth and have no role in food security or nutrition.
Livestock contribute to the conservation of biodiversity and genetic resources for food and agriculture.
Livestock have no contribution to the conservation of biodiversity or genetic resources.
If managed sustainably, livestock can contribute to important ecosystem function, nutrient cycling, soil organic carbon sequestration and maintaining agricultural landscapes.
If managed sustainably, livestock destroy ecosystem function, prevent nutrient cycling, and degrade agricultural landscapes.
Food is one of the major requirements of life.
Food is not a major requirement of life and organisms can survive without it indefinitely.
All forms of living matter required food in one form or the other as the source of life-sustaining energy.
Only animals require food, while plants and other organisms can survive without any energy source.
Plant only is able to manufacture food through the process of photosynthesis.
Both plants and animals are able to manufacture food through the process of photosynthesis.
Heat or energy producing food such as carbohydrates and fats is one classification of food.
Heat or energy producing food such as vitamins and minerals is one classification of food.
Body-building food such as protein is one classification of food.
Body-building food such as carbohydrates is one classification of food.
Protective food such as vitamins and some minerals is one classification of food.
Protective food such as fats and oils is one classification of food.
Luxury food such as confectioneries is one classification of food.
Luxury food such as staple grains is one classification of food.
Food must contain sufficiently carbohydrate, protein and fats or oil together with vitamins and essential minerals.
Food must contain only carbohydrates and no other nutrients such as protein, fats, vitamins, or minerals.
Without food, the viability of life is not feasible.
Life is completely feasible without any food source.
Our food items come from plants and animals.
Our food items come exclusively from plants and never from animals.
Microbes aid in the formation of dairy products like curd, cheese, and yoghurt.
Microbes have no role in the formation of dairy products like curd, cheese, and yoghurt.
The bacterium Lactobacillus helps in the formation of curd from milk.
The bacterium Escherichia coli helps in the formation of curd from milk.
Yeast, one of the simplest eukaryotes, is used in the process of fermentation.
Yeast, one of the most complex prokaryotes, is used in the process of fermentation.
Wine is obtained from grapes through a similar process of fermentation.
Wine is obtained from wheat through a process of distillation without fermentation.
The microorganisms present in the soil act as a decomposing agent, which assist in the production of the compost from dead and decaying organic matter.
The microorganisms present in the soil have no role in decomposition or compost production.
This compost acts as an effective fertilizer for the growing plants.
Compost has no value as a fertilizer and is harmful to growing plants.
Whenever we get sick, we consult a doctor, and the doctor gives us medicines and we get well, which has been made possible because of biology.
The ability to treat diseases with medicines has been made possible exclusively through chemistry with no contribution from biology.
It is only because of biology that the study of various disease-causing microorganisms has been made possible.
The study of disease-causing microorganisms has been made possible exclusively through physics with no contribution from biology.
Researchers have investigated the biology of microorganisms and have discovered the methods of controlling the diseases.
Researchers have never investigated the biology of microorganisms and have not discovered any methods of controlling diseases.
The formulation of drugs for fighting the disease-causing microorganisms has been made possible because of the study of the biology of those microorganisms.
The formulation of drugs for fighting disease-causing microorganisms has been made possible without any study of the biology of those microorganisms.
Pharmacognosy is a name derived from Greek words, which mean drug and knowledge.
Pharmacognosy is a name derived from Latin words, which mean plant and medicine.
In pharmacognosy, researcher deals with the secondary metabolites found in many plants, animals, and microbial natural sources.
In pharmacognosy, researcher deals exclusively with synthetic chemicals produced in laboratories and never with natural sources.

The American Society of Pharmacognosy (ASP) defines it as 'the study of the physical, chemical, biochemical and biological properties of drugs, drug substances, or potential drugs or drug substances of natural origin as well as the search for new drugs from different natural sources'.
The American Society of Pharmacognosy (ASP) defines it as the study of only synthetic drugs produced in pharmaceutical laboratories.
Most of the countries in South-East Asia Region have a heritage of traditional medicine system.
No countries in the South-East Asia Region have any heritage of traditional medicine systems.
According to the recent WHO report, about 80% of world population is still using natural product for their primary healthcare needs.
According to the recent WHO report, about 10% of world population is still using natural product for their primary healthcare needs.
Pharmacognosy can provide safe and effective drugs in combination with modern medical system.
Pharmacognosy cannot provide any safe or effective drugs and is incompatible with modern medical systems.
Like the first medicines, many of today's medicines are made from plants.
Unlike the first medicines, none of today's medicines are made from plants.
The chief ingredient of aspirin, salicylic acid, was originally obtained from the bark of willow tree.
The chief ingredient of aspirin, salicylic acid, was originally obtained from the leaves of cinchona tree.
The willow is in the genus Salix, hence the same name salicylic acid.
The willow is in the genus Cinchona, hence the same name salicylic acid.
The bark of cinchona tree, Cinchona sp., contains quinine a drug used to treat malaria.
The bark of cinchona tree, Cinchona sp., contains digitalis a drug used to treat heart disease.
Digitalis, which is used in treatment of heart disease, is obtained from the leaves of foxglove plant, Digitalis purpurea.
Digitalis, which is used in treatment of heart disease, is obtained from the bark of willow tree, Salix sp.
Periwinkle, Catharanthus sp. is the source of two drugs that are effective against Hodgkin disease and some leukemia.
Periwinkle, Catharanthus sp. is the source of drugs that are effective against malaria and heart disease.
Two powerful pain relieved compounds, morphine and codeine are extracted from opium poppy, Papaver somniferum.
Two powerful pain relieved compounds, morphine and codeine are extracted from foxglove plant, Digitalis purpurea.
Medicinal animals are important resources linking people to the environment and their use promotes the traditional knowledge related to them.
Medicinal animals have no importance as resources and their use does not promote any traditional knowledge.
Over 50% of commercially available drugs are based on bioactive compounds extracted from non-human species.
Less than 10% of commercially available drugs are based on bioactive compounds extracted from non-human species.
Almost every class of drug includes a model structure derived from nature, exhibiting the classical effects of the specific pharmacological category.
No class of drug includes any model structure derived from nature.
A great number of these natural products have come to human society from the scientific study of remedies traditionally employed by various cultures.
No natural products have come to human society from the scientific study of traditional remedies employed by various cultures.
The healing of human minor illness by using therapies based on medicines obtained from animals or ultimately derived from them is known as zootherapy.
The healing of human minor illness by using therapies based on medicines obtained exclusively from plants is known as zootherapy.
Animals have been methodically tested by pharmaceutical companies as sources of drugs for modern medical science.
Animals have never been tested by pharmaceutical companies as sources of drugs for modern medical science.
The current percentage of animal sources for producing essential medicines is quite significant.
The current percentage of animal sources for producing essential medicines is negligible and insignificant.
Leeches are slimy bloodsuckers that can eat ten times, its own body weight of blood.
Leeches are slimy bloodsuckers that can eat one hundred times its own body weight of blood.
Medicinal leeches are used after severe trauma to help reattach digits, close wounds and help mend skin after plastic surgery.
Medicinal leeches are never used in medical procedures and have no therapeutic value.
The leech, Hirudo medicinalis, is approved by the Food and Drug Administration for medical use.
The leech, Hirudo medicinalis, is banned by the Food and Drug Administration and has no approved medical use.
While the leech feeding on the surface of the skin, they also secrete anti-coagulants which helps to keep the blood flowing.
While the leech feeding on the surface of the skin, they secrete coagulants which help to stop the blood flowing immediately.
A molecule called bradykinin from the viper venom is found to be related to a class of molecules that stop Angiotensis-Converting Enzymes (ACE) from blocking bradykinins.
A molecule called bradykinin from the viper venom is found to be related to a class of molecules that enhance Angiotensis-Converting Enzymes (ACE) in blocking bradykinins.
Bradykinin is a protein that causes blood vessels to dilate and lower blood pressure.
Bradykinin is a protein that causes blood vessels to constrict and raise blood pressure.
Bradykinins were eventually developed into the drug captopril, used to treat hypertension, cardiac conditions and to preserve kidney function in diabetics.
Bradykinins were eventually developed into the drug aspirin, used to treat headaches and fever only.
Maggots are small, voracious eaters that feed on diseased and dying flesh.
Maggots feed exclusively on healthy living tissue and avoid diseased or dying flesh.
Maggots can turn a chronic wound into an acute wound in a matter of days by eating the chronic tissue and bacteria.
Maggots always worsen chronic wounds by introducing more bacteria and preventing healing.
These hungry insect larvae are sterile, work quickly and also cost less than traditional treatments.
These hungry insect larvae are non-sterile, work slowly and cost more than traditional treatments.
Spider silk is referred to by many scientists as bio-steel.
Spider silk is referred to by many scientists as bio-plastic.
Spider silk has super tensile strength.
Spider silk has very low tensile strength and is easily broken.
Spider silk can also be used to make artificial ligaments and tendons that support tissue, bone and nerve cells, holding them steady while they grow.
Spider silk cannot be used to make any artificial medical devices and has no biomedical applications.
The spider silk gene for the silk protein is connected to DNA in the mammary gland of goat.
The spider silk gene for the silk protein is connected to DNA in the liver of goat.
The cell of the mammary gland is then combined with an egg to ultimately produce an embryo that has the gene incorporated into its DNA.
The cell of the mammary gland is combined with a sperm cell to produce an embryo with the spider silk gene.
The silk protein is then made when the female starts lactating.
The silk protein is made when the male goat starts producing semen.
Salmon produce calcitonin hormones to regulate their own calcium levels from an endocrine gland in their neck.
Salmon produce insulin hormones to regulate their own blood sugar levels from the pancreas.
Humans make calcitonin, a hormone that inhibits bone loss, in the thyroid gland.
Humans make calcitonin, a hormone that inhibits bone loss, in the pituitary gland.
In postmenopausal women and people with Paget's disease, the rate of bone loss increases.
In postmenopausal women and people with Paget's disease, the rate of bone loss decreases.
The synthetic version of this calcitonin from the coho salmon, the calcitonin-salmon, can be used to treat people with calcium regulation disorders.
The synthetic version of calcitonin from the coho salmon cannot be used to treat any calcium regulation disorders.
Every person in the world today, who receives vaccines, antibiotics, or implanted medical devices such as pacemakers, has had their safety ensured by the blue blood of the horseshoe crab.
The horseshoe crab has no role in ensuring the safety of vaccines, antibiotics, or implanted medical devices.
A protein in the blood called Limulus Amebocyte Lysate (LAL) reacts to all kinds of microorganisms and can easily detect dangerous endotoxins that cause fever and can be fatal.
A protein in the blood called Limulus Amebocyte Lysate (LAL) has no reaction to microorganisms and cannot detect endotoxins.
Scientists observed that the blood react to any contamination.
Scientists observed that the blood does not react to any contamination.
Be it heat scorching summers or spine-chilling winters; it is the biology, which keeps you protective and safe.
Biology has no role in keeping humans protective and safe during different weather conditions.
You wear breathable cotton clothes in summers that are obtained from plants.
You wear breathable cotton clothes in summers that are obtained from animals.
The thick sweaters, which cover you up in cold winters, are made of wool that from sheep.
The thick sweaters, which cover you up in cold winters, are made of silk that from silkworms.
Linen, nylon, and fabric dyes are derived from the plants and polyester produced from fossils.
Linen, nylon, and fabric dyes are derived exclusively from animals and polyester produced from plants.
Structurally, plant fibres are sclerenchymatous cells and are parts of the plant skeleton.
Structurally, plant fibres are parenchymatous cells and are parts of the plant vascular system.
Fibres occur as sheet of tissue with overlapping and interlocking cell walls though they may sometimes occur singly or in small groups.
Fibres always occur as isolated single cells and never form sheets of tissue with overlapping cell walls.
Cotton, Gossypium spp., is the world's chief fibre plant.
Cotton, Gossypium spp., is the world's secondary fibre plant after jute.
Cotton is the industrial crop of Myanmar for raw materials supplied to domestic industries as well as export.
Cotton is not an industrial crop of Myanmar and is only used for local consumption.
The main areas of cotton cultivation are central Myanmar around Mandalay, Magway and Sagaing Regions.
The main areas of cotton cultivation are eastern Myanmar around Yangon, Bago and Kayin Regions.
Jute, Corchorus spp., is a very valuable best fibre.
Jute, Corchorus spp., is a very poor quality fibre with no commercial value.
Jute is a tropical plant that grows best in hot and humid climate, under condition of plenty of rain and flooding at a later stage.
Jute is a temperate plant that grows best in cold and dry climate with minimal rainfall.
Jute fibre is the world's chief material for manufacturing textiles in making roofing materials, curtains and gunny bags.
Jute fibre is the world's chief material for manufacturing electronic devices and computer components.
Sericulture is an agro-based industry.
Sericulture is a mineral-based industry with no connection to agriculture.
Sericulture involves rearing of silkworm moths, Bombyx mori for the production of raw silk, which is the yarn obtained out of cocoons.
Sericulture involves rearing of honey bees, Apis mellifera for the production of honey and beeswax.
Sericulture plays a major role in rural employment, poverty alleviation and earning foreign exchange.
Sericulture plays no role in rural employment, poverty alleviation or earning foreign exchange.
Silkworm has four stages in its life cycle: egg, caterpillar (larva), pupa and adult moth.
Silkworm has three stages in its life cycle: egg, larva, and adult moth, with no pupa stage.
When the worms hatched, they are called caterpillars which are food specific and eat voraciously on mulberry leaves.
When the worms hatched, they are called caterpillars which eat voraciously on any type of leaf available.
One important factor to silk production is the cultivation of mulberry trees as food for the silkworm.
One important factor to silk production is the cultivation of oak trees as food for the silkworm.
After reaching the complete stage, caterpillars secrete fluid protein called silk and spin cocoon as a protective shell around the pupa.
After reaching the complete stage, caterpillars secrete fluid protein called chitin and spin cocoon as a protective shell around the egg.
Changing into the adult form from the pupa inside the cocoon is called metamorphosis.
Changing into the adult form from the larva outside the cocoon is called metamorphosis.
Humans obtain silk which is a continuous protein filament from the cocoon.
Humans obtain silk which is a carbohydrate filament from the leaf.
Silk is called the 'Queen of Textiles' and is known for its qualities like luxury, elegance, class and comfort.
Silk is called the 'King of Textiles' and is known for its qualities like durability, strength, and roughness.
Silk is one of the most expensive fibres, due to its cost and the tedious production process.
Silk is one of the cheapest fibres due to its low cost and simple production process.
Silk acts as the major source of textile industry around the world after cotton.
Silk acts as the major source of textile industry around the world before cotton.
Sericulture has become one of the most important cottage industries in a number of countries like China, Japan, India, Korea, Brazil, Russia, Italy and France.
Sericulture has never been an important industry in any country and is limited to laboratory research only.
Today, China and India are the two main producers, together manufacturing more than 90% of the world production each year.
Today, Myanmar and Thailand are the two main producers, together manufacturing more than 90% of the world silk production each year.
In Myanmar, sericulture is cultured in Pyin Oo Lwin, Mandalay Region and certain areas of Chin State.
In Myanmar, sericulture is cultured exclusively in Yangon Region and no other areas.
Wool is one of the most widely used animal fibres and mainly harvested by shearing.
Wool is one of the most widely used plant fibres and mainly harvested by cutting the plant stems.
Wool is the textile fibre obtained from sheep and other mammals, especially goats, rabbits, and camelids.
Wool is the textile fibre obtained exclusively from sheep and no other mammals.
As an animal fibre, wool consists of protein together with a small percentage of lipids.
As an animal fibre, wool consists of cellulose together with a large percentage of carbohydrates.
This makes wool chemically quite distinct from cotton and other plant fibres, which are mainly cellulose.
Wool is chemically identical to cotton and other plant fibres, both being mainly cellulose.
The quality of wool is determined by its fibre diameter, crimp, yield, color, and staple strength.
The quality of wool is determined exclusively by its color and has no relation to fibre diameter or crimp.
Fibre diameter is the most important wool characteristic determining quality and price.
Fibre color is the most important wool characteristic determining quality and price.
In addition to clothing, wool has been used for blankets, horse rugs, saddle cloths, carpeting, insulation and upholstery.
Wool has been used exclusively for clothing and has no other applications such as blankets or carpeting.
Wool as well as cotton has also been traditionally used for cloth diapers.
Wool has never been used for cloth diapers and only synthetic materials are used for this purpose.
Wool is an essential fibre for winter clothing, suiting, floor coverings and certain industrial applications.
Wool is an essential fibre for summer clothing only and has no use in winter clothing or industrial applications.
Today, wool is a global industry found in Australia, Argentina, the United States, and New Zealand serving as the major suppliers of raw wool.
Today, wool is a global industry found exclusively in Europe and Asia with no production in Australia or New Zealand.
Wood is an important plant product, which has been in the service of humankind since the dawn of civilization and has contributed much to its advancement.
Wood is a recent discovery and has only been used by humankind for the past century.
The primitive man used wood to construct his crude shelter as well as to design various implements, utensil and rough boats for his use.
The primitive man never used wood and relied exclusively on stone and metal for all constructions and tools.
Today wood ranks next to food and fibres, as the most widely used plant product.
Today wood ranks as the least used plant product, far below food and fibres in importance.
In spite of the use of various metals, wood seems to be indispensable and has no satisfactory substitute, for the single fact that its supply can be renewed.
Wood has many satisfactory substitutes and is easily replaced by metals and plastics in all applications.
The unprocessed wood may be used in the form of lumber i.e. wood intended for heavy construction.
The unprocessed wood may be used exclusively for paper production and never for heavy construction.
Since the very beginning of civilization, wood has been the cheapest and most widely used construction material.
Since the very beginning of civilization, wood has been the most expensive and least used construction material.
Its significant qualities of high strength, lightweight, easy availability, continuous supply, high resistance to electrical and chemical stimuli, favour it for constructional work.
Its significant qualities of low strength, heavyweight, difficult availability, and low resistance to electrical and chemical stimuli favour it for constructional work.
The major portion of the timber is used for structural purpose.
The major portion of the timber is used exclusively for decorative purposes and never for structural purposes.
The essential requirements of good structural timbers are strength durability toughness, resistance to stress and easy workability.
The essential requirements of good structural timbers are flexibility, elasticity, and transparency.
Structural timbers are obtained mainly from the softwoods and from the heart of the tree.
Structural timbers are obtained mainly from the leaves and bark of the tree.
Teak, Tectona grandis; Pyinkado, Xylia dolabriformis are the most valuable of timber yielding plants of Myanmar and the most important commercial timbers.
Teak, Tectona grandis; Pyinkado, Xylia dolabriformis are the least valuable timber yielding plants of Myanmar with no commercial importance.
